brief_justification: 4% LIHTC with tax-exempt bonds. Less competitive than 9%. Good for larger senior projects in DeKalb. Not as-of-right but more accessible.
special_notes: Works well for rehab and larger new construction. Combine with HOME and NHTF.

brief_justification: CalHFA permanent loans for seniors, veterans, special needs. Not a grant but excellent below-market financing. RHF track record is strong.
special_notes: Tax-exempt and taxable options available. Combine with LIHTC for full capital stack.

brief_justification: Nearly $250M approved for 45 developments. 2026-2027 round NOW OPEN. Perfect timing for RHF Clark County projects.
special_notes: EXCELLENT OPPORTUNITY - Active round with Nov 25, 2025 deadline. HOME/AAHTF funding.

opportunity_name: Community Housing Capital - Multifamily Construction Loan
mission_program_area: New construction and rehab of multifamily
brief_justification: CDFI offering up to 95% LTV construction financing. Perfect for DeKalb County projects. Gap financing with subsidy in place.
special_notes: Not a grant but flexible CDFI lending. Excellent for Georgia projects with other soft funding.

opportunity_name: Community Housing Capital - Multifamily Permanent Loan
brief_justification: Fixed-rate permanent loans for 20+ unit properties. Lockout periods available. Georgia focus aligns with RHF DeKalb market.
special_notes: CDFI loan product, not grant. Combine with LIHTC for complete capital stack.

opportunity_name: Utah HUD/FHA Section 542(c) Risk Share Loans
brief_justification: FHA-insured permanent loans via UHC. 5+ unit minimum. Below-market rates with 25 bps FHA MIP. Strong for RHF senior projects.
special_notes: Contact mfRiskShare@uthc.org for details.

brief_justification: Major CDFI focused on affordable housing. Invested $27M in 12 developments in Southeast in 2024. National reach covers all RHF markets.
special_notes: Works with LIHTC, HOME, and other public sources. Predevelopment loans available.

opportunity_name: LISC Los Angeles - Affordable Housing Loans
brief_justification: LISC LA has deployed $855M supporting 10,000+ affordable units. Riverside in service area. Predevelopment and construction lending.
special_notes: LISC is certified CDFI with strong California presence. Excellent for gap financing.

brief_justification: UHC authorized $55M in bonds for 255-unit project in 2025. Active bond pipeline. Not a grant but essential financing layer.
special_notes: Combine with 4% federal LIHTC and state credits for full stack.

opportunity_name: Nevada Housing Division - Tax-Exempt Bond Financing
brief_justification: $283M allocated for 2024 bond authority. Strong pipeline of 9+ projects. Clark County is RHF target market.
special_notes: Works with 4% LIHTC. 2024 saw $283M earmarked for affordable housing.

opportunity_name: FHLBank San Francisco - Nevada Targeted Fund
funder_name: Federal Home Loan Bank of San Francisco
brief_justification: $5.1M awarded to 5 Nevada projects creating 270 units in July 2025. Targeted fund specifically for Nevada including Clark County.
special_notes: Nevada-specific fund. Excellent for Clark County senior housing. Apply 2026.

brief_justification: Nevada NHTF allocation for ELI housing. Clark County is priority market for RHF. NV receives minimum $3M allocation.
special_notes: Combine with LIHTC for stronger project financing in Las Vegas market.

brief_justification: Georgia NHTF for ELI rental housing. DeKalb County is RHF market. GA receives minimum $3M allocation for 2025.
special_notes: Work with Georgia DCA on timing with LIHTC applications.

funder_name: HUD via Arizona Department of Housing
brief_justification: Arizona NHTF for Phoenix market. AZ receives minimum $3M allocation. Strong for RHF senior housing projects.
special_notes: Phoenix is high-priority market per AZ housing needs assessment.

brief_justification: Utah NHTF allocation. UT receives minimum $3M for 2025. Gap financing for senior housing developments.
special_notes: Utah Housing Corporation manages both NHTF and state housing credits.

funder_name: Federal Home Loan Bank of Topeka
brief_justification: FHLBank Topeka covers Utah. Round closes August 15, 2025 - still time to apply. Senior housing eligible.
special_notes: ACTIVE OPPORTUNITY for Utah projects. Partner with member bank. August deadline.

brief_justification: NHTF requires 100% units for ELI households. Perfect for RHF low-income senior housing in Riverside. CA allocated $3M+ minimum for 2025.
special_notes: Must meet 30-year affordability period. Excellent gap financing source for Riverside projects.

mission_program_area: New construction and rehab of multifamily
brief_justification: Nevada trust fund for affordable housing gap financing. Real property transfer tax funded. Excellent for Clark County senior projects.
special_notes: Combine with LIHTC and federal HOME funds.

brief_justification: $46M from SHTF in FY2024 budget. Historic investment. Phoenix is key RHF market. Gap financing for senior housing.
special_notes: Governor Hobbs announced $89M for housing in Jan 2024. Strong political support.

mission_program_area: State tax credit for affordable housing
brief_justification: State LIHTC supplements federal credits. Laws 2021 established program. ADOH reserved $2M state credits for 287 units in 2025 round.
special_notes: Stack with federal 4% LIHTC and bonds for full capital structure.

brief_justification: CalHFA subordinate gap financing for 30-120% AMI projects. Strong fit for RHF mixed-income senior developments in Riverside.
special_notes: SB2 funded program. Works well as gap layer with LIHTC and bonds.

brief_justification: HOME is largest federal block grant for affordable housing. 15% CHDO set-aside if RHF qualifies. Excellent for Riverside projects.
special_notes: RHF should pursue CHDO certification in California for set-aside funds.

opportunity_name: FHLBank San Francisco - Affordable Housing Program (AHP)
funder_name: Federal Home Loan Bank of San Francisco
brief_justification: FHLBank SF awarded $49.7M for 31 projects in 2025. Covers RHF's CA, NV, AZ markets. Requires member bank sponsor. 20% units at 50% AMI.
special_notes: 2025 round already awarded. Apply in 2026. Partner with member bank (Wells Fargo, BofA, etc.).

funder_name: Federal Home Loan Bank of Atlanta
brief_justification: FHLBank Atlanta offered $55M in 2024 AHP - record amount. Covers Georgia/DeKalb County. 20% units at 50% AMI required.
special_notes: Partner with member bank for Georgia projects. Watch for 2025 round.

funder_name: HUD - Department of Housing and Urban Development
mission_program_area: Homeless services, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing
brief_justification: Major HUD program for housing providers. RHF qualifies as nonprofit housing developer. Strong fit for senior housing continuum but requires CoC participation. $3.9B total available.
special_notes: Must work through local Continuum of Care collaborative applicant. Strong for CA, NV, AZ, GA, UT markets.

opportunity_name: Georgia DCA Housing Tax Credit Program (9% LIHTC)
brief_justification: 9% LIHTC is highly competitive but excellent equity source. GA DCA awarded $37M in Sept 2025. DeKalb County is RHF market.
special_notes: 2024-2025 QAP amended Feb 2025. Review scoring priorities for senior housing.

mission_program_area: Low-income rental housing for families, seniors
brief_justification: UHC is Utah's allocating agency. 2025 pool is $10M state credits + $12.4M available. Senior housing is priority population.
special_notes: State credits stack with federal. UHC recommended $8.9M in 2025 state credits to projects.

brief_justification: Major federal fund for affordable housing. $246M awarded in FY2024 to 48 organizations. Requires 10x leverage. RHF eligible as nonprofit housing org.
special_notes: Highly competitive - 136 applicants requested $1.06B for $246M available. Strong leverage requirement but excellent for scale.

opportunity_name: California AHSC - Affordable Housing and Sustainable Communities Round 10
mission_program_area: Affordable housing near transit, climate resilience
brief_justification: Massive CA program with $675M+ per round. Perfect for senior housing near transit in Riverside. Highly competitive but excellent fit.
special_notes: Requires transit orientation. Round 9 closed May 2025. Watch for Round 10 NOFA.

opportunity_name: HUD Green and Resilient Retrofit Program (GRRP)
mission_program_area: Energy efficiency, climate resilience for affordable housing
brief_justification: $1B IRA program for affordable housing retrofits. 270 properties, 30,000 units benefited by Nov 2024. Excellent for RHF existing portfolio.
special_notes: Best for existing HUD-assisted properties. RHF likely has qualifying portfolio.

mission_program_area: Green building tools for affordable housing
brief_justification: RFP for Green Communities tools development. Smaller grants but supports sustainable housing practices. Active deadline Nov 21.
special_notes: ACTIVE OPPORTUNITY - deadline TODAY Nov 21. If missed, watch for future rounds.

brief_justification: National network supporting affordable housing. RHF would need to be or partner with NeighborWorks member organization.
special_notes: Consider NeighborWorks membership if not already. Access to training, grants, loans.

opportunity_name: Bank of America Charitable Foundation - Stable Housing
brief_justification: BofA annual housing grants. $5K-50K range below RHF target but good for capacity/programs. 2024 saw 133% increase in senior housing units financed.
special_notes: Smaller grants for programs. BofA Community Development Banking does larger housing financing.

brief_justification: USDA grants for elderly (62+) low-income homeowners. Small grant size ($10K cap). Rural focus limits applicability to RHF urban portfolio.
special_notes: Homeowner program, not rental. May benefit RHF residents transitioning to homeownership.

brief_justification: $55.5M for infrastructure supporting infill housing. Riverside may qualify depending on specific location. Gap funding for infrastructure.
special_notes: Focus is infrastructure (streets, utilities) not housing construction. Complementary to housing funding.

brief_justification: $2B EPA grant to Enterprise coalition for affordable housing decarbonization. Major opportunity for energy retrofits in RHF portfolio.
special_notes: Coalition includes Enterprise, Rewiring America, Habitat, LISC, United Way. Watch for application windows.

brief_justification: $155M available for CDFI FA/TA awards, plus $100M for Housing Production-FA. RHF would need CDFI certification or partner with certified CDFI.
special_notes: Consider CDFI certification pathway or CDFI partnership for multiple funding streams.

funder_name: Georgia DCA + Corporation for Supportive Housing
brief_justification: CSH partnership for supportive housing development. Institute participation helps with LIHTC scoring. 5 teams selected for 2024-2025.
special_notes: Watch for 2025-2026 Institute if RHF interested in supportive housing track.

opportunity_name: Nevada Permanent Supportive Housing Grant Program
brief_justification: AB 310 appropriated $30M for PSH. 12 applications received for round 1. Watch for round 2 if RHF has PSH pipeline.
special_notes: First round closed Nov 2024. Monitor for additional funding rounds.

mission_program_area: Housing affordability, racial equity in housing
brief_justification: Wells Fargo's largest giving area is Housing Affordability. $1B commitment through 2025. $6.3M to Habitat in 2024. Invitation-only process.
special_notes: Build relationship with Wells Fargo community relations. Request invitation to apply.

mission_program_area: Climate resilience, affordable housing, community development
brief_justification: Large implementation grants for disadvantaged communities. Includes housing component. $105M+ per round. Very competitive.
special_notes: Requires community coalition. Housing can be component of larger community plan.

brief_justification: Request for Qualifications for faith-based housing support. Atlanta/Southeast focus includes DeKalb County. RFQ not RFP.
special_notes: Consultants/firms focus. May be useful for RHF faith-based partnerships.

brief_justification: Habitat receives major corporate funding (Wells Fargo $6.3M in 2024). Partnership model rather than direct grants to RHF.
special_notes: Consider partnership on senior homeowner repair programs in RHF markets.

mission_program_area: Housing affordability, health equity, community development
brief_justification: $3.6B foundation focused on urban opportunity. $7M LA PSH commitment in 2024. Strong housing-health nexus interest. Invitation only.
special_notes: Member of Funders for Housing and Opportunity collaborative. Explore relationship building.

opportunity_name: Housing Partnership Network - Renter Advantage Program
brief_justification: $4.4M Wells Fargo grant for renter credit building. HPN membership required. RHF could access for resident services.
special_notes: Not capital funding but resident services. Consider HPN membership benefits.

brief_justification: $217M foundation gave $949K to community organizations. Broad focus but housing is secondary. Smaller grant sizes.
special_notes: National reach. Research specific housing funding priorities.

opportunity_name: Wells Fargo Housing Affordability Breakthrough Challenge
brief_justification: $20M initiative for housing innovation. Large grants ($2-3M) plus 2 years technical assistance. Highly competitive but transformative if won.
special_notes: Requires innovative approach. Best if RHF has scalable model to showcase.

funder_name: John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation
brief_justification: Major foundation supporting housing policy and innovation. Part of Funders for Housing and Opportunity. Invitation-only, strategic focus.
special_notes: Tends to fund policy/advocacy and national intermediaries. May fund direct housing less.

opportunity_name: Conrad N. Hilton Foundation - Homelessness (Los Angeles)
brief_justification: Hilton is largest philanthropic supporter of LA homelessness solutions. $8.5M in Q2 2024 grants. Limited to LA County - not RHF's core market.
special_notes: Focus is LA County homelessness. If RHF expands to LA, strong potential partner.

geographic_restrictions: TX, OK, NM, AR, KS, MO (not RHF states)
brief_justification: Major foundation with $2B assets, gave $5.8M to community housing. Geographic mismatch - focuses on TX/OK region, not RHF states.
special_notes: Only relevant if RHF expands to Texas/Oklahoma region.

brief_justification: $440M foundation with community focus. Gave $1.2M to 30 community/housing orgs. Colorado focus limits applicability.
special_notes: Colorado-focused. Not relevant unless RHF expands there.

opportunity_name: Indian Housing Block Grant Competitive (IHBG-COMP) FY2025
funder_name: HUD - Office of Native American Programs
brief_justification: Limited eligibility - requires tribal affiliation or TDHE status. RHF would need partnership with tribal entity. $150M total available.
special_notes: Only pursue if RHF has existing tribal partnerships in target geographies.

opportunity_name: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) - Indian Tribes
funder_name: HUD - Office of Native American Programs
brief_justification: Federal CDBG for tribes. RHF not directly eligible but could partner. $70M available.
special_notes: Explore partnership opportunities with Arizona tribal entities for senior housing.

geographic_restrictions: Eastern PA, NJ, DE (not RHF states)
brief_justification: Strong grants but geographic mismatch - PA/NJ/DE focus. Not applicable to RHF current markets.
special_notes: Only relevant if RHF expands to Mid-Atlantic region.
